message:
fix(config): validate file-config values before merging into runtime Config

`validate_args` only runs against the parsed CLI `Args`, so a config
file (e.g. `~/.config/aadc/config.toml` or a project-local
`.aadc.toml`) was free to inject values that the CLI rejects:

- `tab_width = 0` slipped through to `expand_tabs`, where the
  modular-arithmetic `tab_width - (col % tab_width)` panicked with a
  divide-by-zero on the first tab character in any input.
- `tab_width > 16` produced absurdly wide tab stops with no warning.
- `max_iters = 0` silently disabled all correction passes (the
  `for iteration in 0..config.max_iters` loop never enters).
- `min_score` outside `0.0..=1.0` either auto-rejected every revision
  (>1.0) or auto-accepted every revision (<0.0).

Add a `validate_file_config` step inside `load_config_file` that runs
the same bounds checks `validate_args` runs on the CLI side, anchored
to the offending file's path so error messages point at the right
location. The post-merge runtime `Config` is then guaranteed valid
without any second-pass validation.

Five regression tests cover each shape: `tab_width = 0`,
`tab_width = 99`, `max_iters = 0`, `min_score` at three
out-of-range values, and a positive case asserting valid values
round-trip cleanly. The tests use `fs::write` rather than the
`Write` trait + `writeln!` macro to sidestep a `clippy::unused_imports`
false positive against `use std::io::Write as _;` on Rust 2024.

cargo test: 213 lib + 27 integration pass.
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings: clean.
cargo fmt --check: clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
